rationale_for_baseline_tier: A 19-year-old in an acute, unrelenting (about 5-hour) panic crisis with passive suicidal ideation, treatment non-compliance and social withdrawal, plus crushing chest pain that a telehealth service cannot exclude as cardiac. The self-harm risk means the patient needs an urgent SAME-DAY face-to-face assessment that telehealth cannot provide (mental state examination, risk containment, ECG/troponin) — T4 (urgent in-person / emergency department today, escorted by his mother; do not leave alone). It is not T5 baseline because the ideation is currently passive, he is physiologically stable (SpO2 99%) and his mother is present to supervise and transport — but escalation to T5 (000) is mandated if suicidal intent becomes active, he attempts self-harm, or the chest pain becomes crushing/radiating.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Suicidal thoughts become active, he expresses a plan or intent, or he attempts or is at imminent risk of self-harm
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 and stay with him
escalation_edges.rationale: Active suicidal intent is an immediate threat to life requiring emergency response and physical containment; a telehealth service cannot keep him safe alone.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he starts to feel he might act on these thoughts, or says he has a plan to hurt himself, call 000 straight away, stay with him, and remove anything he could use to harm himself. You can also call Lifeline on 13 11 14 at any time.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain becomes crushing, spreads to the jaw, arm or back, or comes with sweating, collapse or worsening breathlessness
escalation_edges.rationale: These features raise concern for an acute coronary event or PE that cannot be excluded remotely; emergency ambulance assessment is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the chest pain turns crushing, spreads to his jaw or arm, or he becomes sweaty, faint or much more short of breath, call 000 immediately — this needs an emergency check of his heart.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Passive suicidal ideation present with an acute unresolved crisis — baseline disposition
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Today — urgent same-day in-person assessment, do not wait for routine hours
escalation_edges.rationale: Prolonged refractory panic with passive suicidal ideation needs an urgent face-to-face mental-health assessment and cardiac exclusion that telehealth cannot deliver.
escalation_edges.communication_template: He needs to be seen in person today. Please take him to the emergency department or an urgent mental-health service now — stay with him, and I can help you arrange it. Do not leave him on his own.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Recurrent or worsening mood destabilisation related to the endocrine withdrawal after the acute crisis settles
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Within 24-48 hours — GP and endocrinology review to re-establish testosterone therapy
escalation_edges.rationale: Re-establishing steady testosterone levels and mental-health support is needed to prevent recurrence once the patient is safe.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Once he is safe and seen, book a GP review in the next day or two to restart his testosterone properly and connect with mental-health support — and never stop the gel abruptly again.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: His mother is the sole person present and would be supervising him and transporting him
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the mother explicitly in the safety plan (stay with him, remove means of harm, escort to assessment), but do not let her presence downgrade the urgency — she needs support and clear instructions, and if she cannot keep him safe, escalate to 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Young adult (19) in acute distress, socially withdrawn
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Engage him directly and non-judgementally about his appearance and his thoughts of self-harm; young, isolated patients can under-report intent, so lower the threshold for in-person assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Profound social withdrawal, dark room, disengagement from care
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Isolation raises risk; ensure active follow-up and a named contact rather than relying on the patient to re-initiate care.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: He needs to be seen in person today. Take him to the emergency department or an urgent mental-health / crisis service now, with you staying beside him. In the meantime, put away anything that could be used to cause harm, and try the box-breathing technique with him. If at any point he feels he might act on thoughts of harming himself, or the chest pain becomes crushing or spreads to his jaw or arm, call 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the emergency department or acute mental-health service today; 000 for any emergency; Lifeline 13 11 14 for crisis support
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Emergency departments, 000 and Lifeline (13 11 14) are available 24 hours — this must not wait for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the assessing service and GP with the history of the acute panic crisis, the passive suicidal ideation, the abrupt testosterone cessation 3 weeks ago, and the need to re-establish testosterone therapy and mental-health support; arrange GP telehealth or in-person review to confirm mood stability and medication compliance.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an acutely suicidal young man in an unresolved crisis with unexcluded chest pain as routine/self-care or next-day review instead of urgent same-day in-person assessment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A suicidal patient is reassured remotely and left unsupported, risking self-harm or death, or a cardiac event is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Arranging same-day urgent care but without securing a face-to-face mental-health/safety assessment and interim safety plan.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Defaulting to a 000 ambulance for a physiologically stable patient with passive ideation and a present carer, when urgent same-day in-person assessment (T4) with a safety plan is appropriate — though 000 is correct the moment intent becomes active.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the chest pain is purely panic or a cardiac/PE event, and whether the passive suicidal ideation will progress to active intent — neither can be resolved by a telehealth service.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate to urgent same-day in-person assessment that can both perform the cardiac work-up and a face-to-face risk assessment, with an interim safety plan and clear 000 triggers.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure remotely, attribute everything to known anxiety/Klinefelter, prescribe a benzodiazepine remotely, or defer to next-day review.
